summary: Phase 4 (Mi Legado / Family Tree) tested. Backend: 16/22 pytest tests PASSED (auth guards, preferencias sonido + arbol_publico CRUD with 400 on invalid sonido, familiar CREATE+GET+ /mios list, cross-user 403 on PUT/DELETE, privacy public-tree visible to other user, privacy private-tree blocked to other user, historia required-content 400, GEDCOM empty/invalid 400). 6/22 FAILED — all caused by ONE root cause: middleware/auth.js sets req.userId = user._id (Mongoose ObjectId), and the new /api/familiares/* and historia/foto routes compare `familiar.usuario_id.toString() !== req.userId` (string !== ObjectId is always TRUE). Result: the OWNER is forbidden from updating/deleting/uploading-foto/adding-historias on their own familiar, and from viewing /api/familiares/usuario/:ownId when arbol is private. SEPARATE bug: GEDCOM parser picks 'first INDI without FAMC' as root (I2 Father in the canonical sample), so familiares=[] is returned for the spec sample (per spec the FIRST INDI should be root → expected 2 familiares padre+madre). Frontend: /mi-legado page renders correctly inside PageShell — header 'Árbol del legado', subtitle, Importar GEDCOM + Agregar familiar buttons, SideRail 'Mi legado' (feather icon) link, tree SVG with cameo dorado circles. AddFamiliarModal opens with photo-upload circle, nombre, apellido, parentesco (24 options — model enum is 24 not 25 as the spec said), género, nacimiento, defunción, lugar, ocupación, bio. Could not complete detail-modal / preferencias / GEDCOM-modal flows because: (a) AddFamiliarModal does not close on Escape and its backdrop intercepts subsequent clicks (UX bug), (b) clicking the first tree node (which is the root/self cameo) does not open the detail modal — only familiar nodes do, my script picked the wrong index.

critical.endpoint: PUT /api/familiares/:id, DELETE /api/familiares/:id, POST /api/familiares/:id/foto, POST/DELETE /api/familiares/:id/historias/*, GET /api/familiares/usuario/:userId (own private)
critical.issue: Owner gets 403 on their own resource. Root cause in /app/backend/middleware/auth.js line 33 — req.userId is assigned user._id (ObjectId), but Phase 4 routes compare `familiar.usuario_id.toString() !== req.userId` (string vs ObjectId) which is ALWAYS true. Existing Phase 1 code at server.js:354,381 correctly does `req.userId.toString()`. Fix: either change middleware to `req.userId = user._id.toString();` (preferred, matches existing pattern usage) OR add `.toString()` to all Phase 4 comparisons (lines 1160, 1218, 1244, 1265, 1289, 1312).

Parser returns 0 familiares for the canonical spec GEDCOM. /app/backend/utils/gedcomParser.js line 133: `const root = individuos.find(i => !i.fields.FAMC) || individuos[0];` — for the spec sample (I1 has FAMC, I2/I3 don't) this picks I2 (Father) as root, then I2 has no FAMC so no ancestors are derived → familiares=[] → 400. Per spec note 'The first INDI is treated as root'; the fallback strategy is inverted. Fix: change to `const root = individuos[0];` (and let buscarAbuelos handle the case where the root itself has no FAMC).

minor.issue: Invalid parentesco returns 500 (mongoose ValidationError caught by generic catch), not 400. Add explicit enum check before .save() or convert ValidationError → 400.

ui_bugs.issue: Escape key does not close modal; backdrop intercepts clicks on outside elements (e.g. import-gedcom-btn) until user clicks the X. Blocks subsequent interactions in test scripts and real users who press ESC.
Missing data-testid='familiar-genero-input' on the género select (spec lists it). All other inputs have testids.

When multiple familiares share the same parentesco (e.g. two 'padre' entries), their cameos render at the SAME x,y coordinates causing visually overlapping circles + stacked text labels (e.g. 'TEST_PadreSoto' overlapping 'TEST_Mod'). Position function should fan out siblings horizontally within their row.

integration_issues.flow: Familiar mutate (edit, delete, upload foto, add historia)
integration_issues.issue: All owner mutations fail with 403 from backend due to auth.js bug above. The UI buttons therefore appear broken for the legitimate owner.

Cannot be verified end-to-end: backend parser bug returns 400 for the canonical sample, so 'Importar GEDCOM' will always show error toast for typical Ancestry/MyHeritage exports that follow the structure of the spec sample.

issues: Sibling cameos overlap when parentesco is shared (no horizontal fan-out).

action_items: CRITICAL: Fix middleware/auth.js — set req.userId = user._id.toString() (or add .toString() to all 6 Phase 4 owner-check sites in server.js: 1160, 1218, 1244, 1265, 1289, 1312).
CRITICAL: Fix utils/gedcomParser.js line 133 — use `individuos[0]` as root unconditionally (spec: first INDI is root).
Convert mongoose ValidationError on parentesco enum into a 400 response.
AddFamiliarModal: add ESC-key close handler and click-on-backdrop close.
Add data-testid='familiar-genero-input' to the género select.
Tree layout: horizontally distribute siblings sharing the same parentesco so cameos don't overlap.
critical_code_review_comments: server.js continues to grow (now 1366 lines, +244 since iter 4). Phase 4 added ~250 lines of route handlers for familiares + preferencias to a single monolithic file. Strongly recommend extracting routes/familiares.js, routes/preferencias.js. Maintenance & merge-conflict risk is now substantial.

Phase 4 owner-check pattern `familiar.usuario_id.toString() !== req.userId` differs from Phase 1 pattern `.toString() !== req.userId.toString()`. This drift is exactly what caused the 403 bug. Recommend a helper: `function isOwner(doc, req) { return doc.usuario_id.toString() === String(req.userId); }`.
gedcomParser.js: the duplicate-removal `seen` Set keys on `${parentesco}-${nombre}-${apellido}` will collapse two siblings with the same name + same parentesco. Edge case but documented.

MiembroFamiliar.parentesco enum hard-codes 24 values. If the UI ever wants to surface 'tatarabuelo' or similar, this must be edited in 3 places (model, parser, UI dropdown). Consider a shared constants module.

Foto upload: no check that uploads/familiares directory exists at startup. multer disk-storage will silently fail if the directory is missing on a fresh deploy. Add fs.mkdirSync({recursive:true}) at boot.

Privacy: /api/familiares/usuario/:userId leaks 'es_publico' flag back to caller even when 403 path was avoided; fine for /mios. But note that the dueño's 'nombre' and 'usuario' are returned even though selection didn't ask for them — minor leak.

success_rate.frontend: ~70% (UI shell + AddFamiliarModal open + tree render verified; detail-modal + preferencias + gedcom-modal UI paths blocked by modal-backdrop UX issue, not verified end-to-end)

test_credentials: keilin@chronos.com / chronos123 and arqueo@chronos.com / chronos123 (both work)
seed_data_creation: Test created TEST_-prefixed familiares for keilin during the run. Cleanup fixture auto-removes them before & after the module. A handful of TEST_* nodes pre-existed from earlier interrupted runs and were cleaned up; one such node 'TEST_PubMember' is owned by keilin with arbol_publico flipping during privacy tests — left in tree at end of run because cleanup runs once per module.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 4 test file: /app/backend/tests/test_phase4_legado.py (22 tests, structured by class: AuthGuard, Preferencias, FamiliaresCRUD, Privacy, Historias, FotoUpload, GedcomImport). Run with: `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_phase4_legado.py -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/phase4_results.xml`. Once the auth.js + gedcomParser.js bugs are fixed, all 22 should pass. For UI retest, dismiss AddFamiliarModal via its close X (not ESC) before clicking other buttons. Tree node[0] is the root/self cameo (Keilin) — to click a familiar use node[1+] or query by `[data-testid^='tree-node-']:not(.tree-node-root)`. PreferenciasModal is reached via Profile → 'Editar legado' button → 'Sonido y privacidad' button (data-testid='open-preferencias-btn'). Sonido options preview-play on click via /utils/chronosSound.js variants cuerno/lira/campana/silencio.
rca of the issue: ROOT CAUSE 1 (6 failing tests): /app/backend/middleware/auth.js line 33 assigns `req.userId = user._id` — a Mongoose ObjectId instance. The new Phase 4 routes in /app/backend/server.js compare `familiar.usuario_id.toString() !== req.userId` (lines 1160, 1218, 1244, 1265, 1289, 1312). Since string `'68...'` is never strictly-equal to ObjectId('68...'), the comparison is ALWAYS true → 403 for the legitimate owner. Phase 1 routes at lines 354/381 use `.toString()` on BOTH sides, which is why those work. REPRO: login keilin → POST /api/familiares (succeeds 201) → PUT /api/familiares/:newId with same session (returns 403 instead of 200). MITIGATION: one-line fix to middleware — `req.userId = user._id.toString();` — restores all 6 endpoints to working state with no other code changes needed (since existing toString-on-both-sides callers are tolerant of either type). ROOT CAUSE 2 (1 failing test): /app/backend/utils/gedcomParser.js line 133 selects root as `individuos.find(i => !i.fields.FAMC) || individuos[0]` which for the spec's canonical sample picks I2 (Father, no FAMC) over I1 (intended chronicler with FAMC pointing at F1). Because I2 has no FAMC, no parents/abuelos can be derived and the duplicate-removal pass yields []. REPRO: node -e "require('./utils/gedcomParser').parseGedcomToFamiliares(SAMPLE, 'x')" → [].  MITIGATION: change to `const root = individuos[0];` per spec ('The first INDI is treated as root'). With this fix, the canonical sample produces 2 familiares (padre + madre) as the spec promises.
